questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which statement about galaxies is correct?
options: A galaxy is a single star with many planets around it.
A galaxy is smaller than a solar system.
A galaxy can exist only inside Earth's atmosphere.
A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.correctAnswer: A galaxy is a large collection of stars held together by gravity.
questions.explanation: Galaxies contain billions of stars, gas and dust, all held together by gravity.
questions.senNote: Galaxies contain billions of stars, gas and dust, all held together by gravity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Recall the life cycle of a Sun-like star
questions.question: What happens to a star like the Sun after the red giant stage?
options: It becomes a supernova and then a neutron star.
It turns straight into a black hole.
It becomes a main-sequence star again.
It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
questions.correctAnswer: It forms a planetary nebula and then becomes a white dwarf.
questions.explanation: A Sun-like star becomes a red giant, then a planetary nebula, and finally a white dwarf.
questions.senNote: A Sun-like star becomes a red giant, then a planetary nebula, and finally a white dwarf.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Why are optical telescopes often placed on mountains or in space?
options: Gravity is weaker there so the telescopes can see further.
The telescopes need warmer temperatures to work properly.
There is more sound in the air to carry the signals.
There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.correctAnswer: There is less atmosphere to distort or absorb the light.
questions.explanation: Less atmosphere means clearer observations because there is less absorption and distortion of light.
questions.senNote: Less atmosphere means clearer observations because there is less absorption and distortion of light.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Why do astronauts appear weightless in orbit?
options: There is no gravity at all where satellites orbit.
Their masses become zero once they leave the atmosphere.
The spacecraft pushes them upwards harder than Earth pulls them down.
They are in free-fall while moving forwards around Earth.
questions.correctAnswer: They are in free-fall while moving forwards around Earth.
questions.explanation: Astronauts and the spacecraft are both falling together around Earth, so they appear weightless.
questions.senNote: Astronauts and the spacecraft are both falling together around Earth, so they appear weightless.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What does red-shift suggest about many distant galaxies?
options: They are becoming cooler than all nearby stars only.
They are moving towards us faster than light.
They have stopped emitting electromagnetic waves.
They are moving away from us.
questions.correctAnswer: They are moving away from us.
questions.explanation: Red-shift shows that light has stretched, which happens when a source is moving away from us.
questions.senNote: Red-shift shows that light has stretched, which happens when a source is moving away from us.
